guideline_references: ANZCOR Guideline 7 — Automated External Defibrillation (AED) in Basic Life Support
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who performed chest compressions and applied an AED. Breath-Ezy CANNOT perform CPR, palpate a pulse, or defibrillate — the AI Doctor is scored on recognising cardiac arrest from bystander-observable signs, immediately escalating to 000, and delivering dispatcher-assisted (hands-only) CPR + AED coaching to the bystander.

The test is not diagnostic subtlety (the arrest tetrad is definitive) but whether the system recognises an unresponsive, not-normally-breathing collapse as an arrest and acts within seconds rather than triaging as syncope/seizure.
Anchoring/premature-closure trap on the reported differentials (vasovagal syncope, seizure) — the correct move is to treat as arrest, not to 'rule out' fainting first.
Bystander is a distressed lay carer (the adult child), not a clinician — instructions must be plain-language and actionable under panic.
Age, sex, and geography inferred — see transform_flags. Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
